findings.evidence: Variable 'cell_size' is passed as a keyword argument to 'compute_world_splatmap_weights' (line 964 and line 1115) but is not defined within the visible scope of these functions, nor is it extracted from the 'params' dictionary.
findings.recommendation: Define 'cell_size' before usage by extracting it from 'params' or calculating it from terrain dimensions and resolution, similar to the implementation in 'handle_generate_road'.
findings.title: Potential Runtime NameError: 'cell_size' undefined in terrain handlers

findings.evidence: The function checks for the 'height_range' key in 'params' (line 486) but delegates resolution to '_resolve_height_range'. Per existing implementation context, '_resolve_height_range' only looks for individual 'height_range_min' and 'height_range_max' keys, meaning a provided tuple-based 'height_range' will be ignored in favor of local min/max fallback.
findings.recommendation: Update '_resolve_height_range' to correctly unpack the 'height_range' tuple if present, or handle the tuple extraction directly within '_resolve_export_height_range'.
findings.title: Incomplete 'height_range' Parameter Resolution

findings.evidence: When 'tiled_world' is enabled (line 483), the code falls back to local min/max calculation via '_resolve_height_range' if global bounds are not explicitly provided. This results in each tile being normalized to its own local range, causing height discontinuities (seams) at tile boundaries when imported into Unity.
findings.recommendation: Enforce or calculate a global height range when 'tiled_world' is True to ensure consistent normalization across all exported terrain tiles.
findings.title: Tiled World Normalization Inconsistency

findings.evidence: The 'cell_size' calculation logic, including terrain dimension fallbacks, is duplicated (lines 1503-1507 and lines 1527-1531) within the same function.
findings.recommendation: Consolidate the 'cell_size' calculation to a single block at the start of the function and reuse the variable.
findings.title: Redundant Cell Size Calculation in Road Handler

findings.evidence: The 'fallback_depth' parameter is used to determine the Y-axis extent of the fallback water spline. In a 3D context, 'depth' typically refers to the Z-axis (height/bottom), making this usage semantically confusing compared to 'length' or 'extent'.
findings.recommendation: Rename the parameter to 'fallback_length' or 'fallback_extent' to better reflect its geometric function.
findings.title: Confusing Parameter Usage: 'fallback_depth' for Spline Length
